rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE MANUEL VILLAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/06/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The 04/25/2022 supplement that "corrects the account" must be filed as an amendment and noticed. [CRC 7.3, 7.53]. The summary of account alleges the account covers July 11, 2019 to December 15, 2021. Accounts must begin at date of death of Decedent. [LRSF 14.34]. Property on hand at beginning of account must match the I&A's showing Decedent's assets at date of death. In the Supplement the settlement proceeds should not be in property on hand at beginning of account because the settlement proceeds were received after Decedent died. [Probate Code 1061 et seq.] 2) Please file the closing escrow statement. 3) Please itemize and file receipts for all of petitioner Mercedes Diaz's request for reimbursement. If receipts are not available the court may not grant the reimbursement request. 4) Please explain page 4 paragraph 16 alleging the property on hand is $2,269,484.71 of which is $753,984.71 is cash. This does not appear to match the account. 5) Please explain page 5 paragraph 20 as there is no Will as petitioners alleged Decedent died intestate. 6) Please provide the court order authorizing payment of attorney fees to Samuel G. Broyles. If there is no court order please provide the legal authority/cite probate code sections for hiring and paying Samuel G. Broyles. Please address CRC 7.700 (a) and (b). Please file invoices from the attorney. 7) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] 8) Please explain why $636,345.29 is kept in a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. Please explain whether this is a breach of the personal representatives fiduciary duty. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/23/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID BRILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent was residing at a care facility in Oakland, Alameda County, at the time of his death. Probate Code section 7051 provides in relevant part that the proper county for proceedings concerning administration of the decedent's estate is the county in which the decedent was domiciled. The petitioner alleges that the decedent's domicile remained in San Francisco County, despite his physical residence in Alameda County. 2) The decedent's holographic will provides that: "If my mother does not survive then I give devise and bequeath my estate both real and personal to my brother Victor Brill. If Victor Brill does not survive such distribution then I leave all to his issue." The decedent's mother predeceased him. Victor Brill survived the decedent, but died in January 2022. Victor Brill had only one child, Elliott Brill. Elliott Brill received notice and nominated the petitioner as administrator. Elliott Brill has three minor children who have not received direct notice under CRC 7.51(d). Section 8441(b) provides in relevant part that "a person who takes under the will has priority (for appointment as administrator with the will annexed) over a person who does not." Section 245 provides in relevant part that "where a will that expresses no contrary intention provides for issue or descendants to take without specifying the manner, the property to be distributed shall be distributed in the manner provided in Section 240." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 for more information.
